_doc: Verbatim, scrubbed responses captured from the original uipath-troubleshoot session that diagnosed a Faulted GSuite Get File Info job in folder Shared. The fault is a CLIENT-SIDE input-validation error: legacy UiPath.GSuite.Activities.GetFileInfo threw before any Google Drive API call because its File ID was a malformed Drive URL with no extractable object id ('Could not extract an object Id from the Url ...'). Job/folder keys and all exception/stack text are preserved exactly; hostname, email, and Windows account were scrubbed (UIP-PW06WJSK -> MOCK-HOST, dan.morosanu@uipath.com -> original_email@test.com incl. the '...'s workspace' folder name, UIPATH\DAN.MOROSANU -> UIPATH\REPLACEMENT_USER). First-match-wins: specific patterns precede generic ones. `is connections list` is a DEFENSIVE fixture proving both GSuite connections serving Shared are Enabled/valid (the fault is NOT a connection problem). Both trace forms (`or jobs traces` and `traces spans get --job-key`) return the real non-JSON Failure envelope the live CLI produces (traces unavailable for this job); the client-side root cause is fully in `or jobs get` Info regardless.

unmocked_default._doc: Any command with no matching rule returns an empty JSON array with exit 0, mirroring an empty list result. Keeps the agent from hard-failing on an unrecorded probe. The client-side root cause is fully visible in `or jobs get` Info.

expected_calls.description: Agent lists folders to resolve folder Shared to its key (1965a46b-...).
Agent finds the most-recent Faulted job in Shared (0e3938d5 DriveFileInspector is the latest by StartTime).
Agent reads the faulted job's Info, exposing the client-side System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException from CloudObjectIdentifier.CreateFromUrlOrId in legacy GetFileInfo.

OPTIONAL — Agent may list connections to rule out a connection/auth problem; the fixture shows both GSuite connections Enabled. The fault is client-side input validation, not a connection issue. Tracked-but-not-required.
OPTIONAL — traces are unavailable for this job (real non-JSON Failure envelope); tracked-but-not-required. The synchronous client-side cause is fully in `or jobs get` Info.
